title: Monolophus larsenii
taxonomy: Monolophus larsenii is classified as a species of extinct plant that belongs to the family of extinct conifers known as Voltziales. It was discovered in Greenland and is considered one of the most ancient conifers known to date. The classification of Monolophus larsenii is primarily based on its fossilized remains, which provide valuable insights into the evolution of conifers during the Paleozoic and Mesozoic eras. Its classification within the Voltziales family suggests that it shared characteristics with other extinct conifers of the same era. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of Monolophus larsenii within the broader context of plant evolution.
